title: Cornyn’s Defeat Fuels Tensions With President Trump in Senate G.O.P.
summary: Senators are angry President Trump turned on a respected former leader whom they consider a loyal Republican. Now Mr. Trump faces resistance from his own embittered ranks.

title: Paxton’s Texas Victory Opens a New Front in the Battle for the Senate
summary: Many Democrats and some Republicans said the scandal-plagued Ken Paxton’s victory could turn Texas into a battleground state that will determine Senate control.

title: A Blue Texas May Be More Than a Dream for Democrats
summary: Ken Paxton’s victory for the Republican nomination and a big shift among Hispanic voters have put a Senate seat within reach.

title: Ken Paxton Victory Tests Whether Texas Republicans Can Reunite for November
summary: Republican voters made a familiar bet, that Texas is conservative enough that any Republican, even the most conservative, will beat a Democrat.

title: A MAGA Victory in Texas, and the Trump Administration’s New Ebola Plan
summary: Plus, Iran begins lifting an internet blackout.

title: Oil Prices Fall as Uneasy Truce Holds Between U.S. and Iran
summary: Traders are weighing signals that a more lasting agreement may be within reach against recent flare-ups in hostilities.

title: After 88 Days of Censored News, TV and Chat, Iranians Are Coming Back Online
summary: The government is letting people connect with the world after a near-total internet shutdown. But not everyone has access, and those who do wonder how long it will last.

title: A Quiet Pacific Village Becomes China’s Security Testing Ground
summary: When a remote Pacific village asked for help with rowdy youth, the Chinese police arrived with a surveillance system. Then came the backlash.

title: Cubans Deported to Mexico Live a Precarious Existence, Report Finds
summary: A Human Rights Watch report found that many Cubans deported to Mexico by the Trump administration are living in an “indefinite legal limbo” and struggling to get by.

title: Two of America’s Thorniest Political Issues Are Dividing an Arizona Town
summary: Marana, Ariz., is used to placid local politics, but the town, in a congressional swing district held by Juan Ciscomani, has been convulsed by two national issues, A.I. and immigration detention.

title: The Battle Over Trump’s Immigration Crackdown Comes to a Newark ICE Facility Parking Lot
summary: For five days, activists have gathered outside a detention center in New Jersey to denounce conditions inside. Federal officials have rejected those concerns.

title: The Best Restaurants in San Francisco
summary: From world-class Korean barbecue to Mission-style burritos to quintessential California cuisine, here are our current favorites.

title: The Best Restaurants in Atlanta
summary: There’s no shortage of Southern virtuosity in the 404, but don’t miss the outstanding Indian, Italian and Thai cooking — or the amazing wings from an unexpected spot.

title: The Best Restaurants in Portland, Ore.
summary: The dining capital of the Pacific Northwest remains one of America’s great culinary cities.

title: Trump Administration to Send Americans Exposed to Ebola to Kenya
summary: In past outbreaks, Americans exposed to the virus were sent home to be treated in state-of-the-art facilities. The Trump administration has already flown some U.S. citizens to Europe for treatment.

title: Crypto Firms and Automakers Are Looking to Open Banks, at Trump’s Urging
summary: Urged on by the White House and a reduction in regulatory oversight, crypto companies and automakers are among those that have applied for banking charters.

title: The $400 Million Showdown Between a Billionaire and a California Mayor
summary: The owner of the Los Angeles Rams and the City of Inglewood are in a dispute over Hollywood Park and SoFi Stadium, which is about to host World Cup matches.

title: The Viruses Causing New Outbreaks Are Much Less Familiar to Science
summary: The types of Ebola and hantavirus panicking officials are very different from the species identified decades ago, raising new questions about how to respond.

title: How Delta Steered Around Airline Industry Chaos
summary: The carrier has become the country’s most profitable by catering to affluent travelers, but it is facing stiffer competition from United.

title: Xi Jinping Quit Smoking. China Still Cannot.
summary: China’s tobacco monopoly has become so financially vital to the government that even its powerful leader has failed to curb the country’s smoking habit.

title: Why Is TikTok in This Book from 2006?
summary: For decades, publishers have swapped out cultural references in new editions of books to appeal to younger readers. Fans aren’t always thrilled.

title: Hunting a ‘Frankenfish’ With a Bow and Arrow
summary: Invasive snakeheads are proliferating in waterways from New York to Florida. Wildlife officials around the Chesapeake Bay are recommending a solution: high-powered compound bows.

title: Writing Is Fundamental to How We Think
summary: A.I. can be a crutch that hurts our ability to think creatively.

title: Time Gave Me Back What Prison Took Away
summary: A formerly incarcerated writer reflects on how the prison system didn’t foster change, but befriending time did.

title: War. Heat. Cigarettes.
summary: Updates on stories from around the world.

title: Jimmy Kimmel Slams Trump for Missing His Son’s Wedding
summary: “It was a small ceremony with about 40 guests, none of whom were his father,” the late-night host said of Donald Trump Jr.’s wedding in the Bahamas over the weekend.

title: Teachers Union Urges Schools to Curb A.I. Chatbots and Screen Time
summary: The American Federation of Teachers recommended “no screens” at all for those in second grade or younger, and no A.I. chatbots for students in elementary school.

title: Go Ask Alice Why Tech Start-Ups Are Spending Big on Hype Videos
summary: A Mad Hatter and a giant rabbit sit around a table discussing an A.I. start-up. This is normal behavior around the Bay Area these days.

title: Minneapolis Police Chief Brian O’Hara Resigns After Investigation
summary: The chief, Brian O’Hara, began leading the department in 2022 as it reeled in the aftermath of the police killing of George Floyd.

title: For Years, Democrats Refused to Primary Him. This Year Is Different.
summary: Thomas DiNapoli, the state comptroller in New York since 2007, now faces his first primary challenge, as two Democrats hope to capitalize on anti-incumbent fervor.
